title: Chodo Habat

{{Quote|I am a priest of my people, an adept in the Force.|Chodo Habat|<ref name="KOTOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
'''Chodo Habat''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Force Adept]], and leader of the [[Chodo Habat's herd|herd]] that oversaw the [[Telosian Restoration Project]] after the [[Bombing of Telos IV]]. He was a respected spiritual leader among his people because of his ability to feel [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. He helped [[Meetra Surik]] regain some of her own [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] in exchange for her help in dealing with [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] and local [[Exchange]] boss [[Loppak Slusk]], whose combined action of sabotage had brought the Restoration project to a halt. Eventually, his efforts made the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] atmosphere suitable for life again, after it had been turned into acidic vapor by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] bombardment. Twenty other worlds would benefit from his success.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Biography==
===Restoration of Telos IV===
{{Quote|Telos needs new ecosystems if the reclamation efforts on the planet are to succeed.|Bao-Dur on Onderon|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
Ithorians were renowned for their ability to stabilize ecospheres. Habat sought to create new life on Telos by importing wildlife from planets rich in food chains such as [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], and [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], but as the contract disputes with Czerka continued, and Onderon experienced the political schism split between [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia's]] supporters and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]]'s, the arrangement was compromised. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] blockade that followed the [[Blockade of Onderon]], in which the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' was attacked by Onderonian [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] under [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tobin (Human)|Tobin]], meant long delays for both the diplomats managing the trade, and the merchants whose beast cages were filling up the streets of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] since passage in and out of the city was choked by the introduction of [[starport visa]]s. With Talia's triumph over Vaklu, however, Onderon remained within the Republic, and the work of Chodo could continue to rely on their natural resources.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

===Competition with Czerka===
[[File:JanaLorso.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Czerka Executive [[Jana Lorso]]]]
{{Quote|I don't think Habat even realizes that at their current pace, they will run out of funding before even half of the restoration zones are up and running.|Jana Lorso|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
When Republic relations with Onderon began to deteriorate, increasing the purchase and transport costs of biological specimens, Czerka gained a foothold in contract negotiations for the Restoration project and wrested control of a number of Restoration Zones on the planet's surface from the Ithorians. They discovered that the surface of Telos was covered with military facilities that were either destroyed or abandoned during the Sith attack, and as part of the Republic contract, Lorso was allowed to use those resources to help fund and support the project. In the long run, if the Republic decided to start restoring other war-torn worlds, the powerful corporation hoped to integrate itself into the process and gain new markets for Czerka products. Habat himself admitted that without Meetra Surik's intervention, his [[Chodo Habat's herd|herd]] would have had no measure of success in dealing with them.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

===Infrastructure droids===
[[File:Gotofull.jpg|120px|thumb|[[G0-T0]]]]

For the restoration of Telos, Chodo also relied on infrastructure [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to plan and manage the project. The first, one of [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech's]] [[G0-T0 infrastructure planning system]]s commissioned by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Tol Cressa]] in [[3955 BBY]], was lost the very first day it arrived on [[Citadel Station]]. The [[Telos Security Force|TSF]] conducted a thorough investigation into the matter but was unable to find the reason for its disappearance, however they excluded the droid was stolen as some among Chodo's herd suspected. Czerka was also suspected to be responsible, but in truth [[G0-T0]] had left by its own choice. A second, somewhat lesser droid was acquired by the Ithorians to take its place, and was escorted to the [[Citadel Station Ithorian Compound|Ithorian Compound]] by [[Meetra Surik]] at Chodo's request, before it could be stolen by [[Exchange]] thugs hired by Czerka.

===Cooperation with Bao-Dur===
[[File:BaoDurPortrait.jpg|thumb|left|120px|[[Bao-Dur]]]]
{{Quote|He is a friend, and may be trusted.|Chodo Habat on Bao-Dur|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
When [[Citadel Station]] was developing the shield network that allowed the Ithorians to purify and reseed small portions of the surface, they worked with a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] ex-military engineer named [[Bao-Dur]]. Bao-Dur designed and oversaw the installation of the shield system's planet-side components, and when the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' was seized by the [[Handmaiden Sisters]], Chodo told Surik to seek him out on Czerka-held Restoration Zone [[RZ-0031]], confident that his help would have been invaluable. On the planet's surface, Bao-Dur was amazed at the work of the Ithorians, and ever upset at Czerka's attempts at destroying it. He was actually there to sabotage their operations in the hopes of driving Czerka from the Restoration Zone, which had begun deteriorating within weeks since their arrival.

===Cooperation with Meetra Surik===
While Surik was still being kept under house arrest by the [[Telos Security Force]] for her involvement in the destruction of the [[Peragus Mining Facility]], Chodo sent his primary representative, [[Moza]], to meet her and ask for her [[Skirmishes on Citadel Station|assistance]].
{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
Surik agreed to negotiate a truce with [[Loppak Slusk]] as well as rid Chodo's herd of Czerka. She thwarted Czerka Executive [[Jana Lorso]], who sought to steal their latest infrastructure [[Droid/Legends|droid]], and seized her administrative assistant [[B-4D4-GE3]] to obtain compromising information from the Czerka mainframe for Chodo to expose to the [[Telosian Council]]. Later, Surik would save Chodo and his herd from a squad of mercenaries hired by Lorso in [[Skirmish in the Ithorian Compound|retaliation]].

Habat presented Surik with the heirloom of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] once of his herd, a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] component she would eventually use to construct her own weapon. He also allowed her to use one of their [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|orbital shuttles]] to descend to the planet's surface.

During the [[Battle of Telos IV]], Chodo met Surik one last time on [[Citadel Station]] and told her of his herd's progress since Czerka's ban from contract negotiations for the Restoration project. Even as the Sith attacked, Chodo would not leave the station, but insisted Moza did for the sake of the other war-torn planets that would have need for his herd.
{{Endgame}}

Nearly four thousand years later, the [[Chodo Habat Parkway]] would exist on Telos IV.<ref name="Jedi—The Dark Side 2">[[Jedi — The Dark Side 2|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 2]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Chodo Habat was a kind and gentle being who greatly respected life. He wished dearly to see Telos filled with life again, and was worried when Czerka Corporation moved onto the station and began interfering in the restoration project. As a Force Adept, he could sense the terrible wound within Surik, and offered to heal her if she aided him.
{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
Surik complied, and Chodo did as he promised, and also presented Surik with a component from a lightsaber belonging to a Jedi from his herd.
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
If the player sides with Jana Lorso, then Chodo and Moza will refuse to speak with them. It is possible, however, to work for Chodo yet still smuggle some of his possessions to [[Samhan Dobo]], the Exchange [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] on Citadel Station.

Surik would have received the first fixture for her lightsaber from Lorso instead of Chodo, if she sided with Czerka.

==Appearances==
